question_text: Which scenario best demonstrates the relationship between particle arrangement and the properties of a state of matter?

options.label: A
options.text: Ice is hard because its particles are tightly packed in fixed positions with strong forces between them

options.label: B
options.text: Water is a liquid because it contains more particles than ice

options.label: C
options.text: Steam is a gas because its particles are smaller than water particles

options.label: D
options.text: All three states have particles arranged randomly in the same way

explanation: The hardness and rigidity of ice comes directly from how its particles are arranged—they're locked in fixed positions with strong attractive forces holding them in place. This arrangement is what gives solids their characteristic properties of fixed shape and volume.
